title: The Ribbiting Tale of a Lily Pad Labour Dispute

sections.title: Talking to Marcellus without the requirements to start

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Hey! I didn't say you could touch my farming patch, did I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You! I bet you're with them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Them?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: The frogs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frogs? What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Ah, I see. So you're going to play dumb are you? Well, be gone! I won't fall for your lies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You didn't go near those frogs yet, did you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: I can tell. If you had, you'd be dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then... Well assuming that I survive, I'll be back shortly to let you know what I've found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You! I bet you're with them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Them?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: The frogs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frogs? What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Ah, I see. So you're going to play dumb are you? Well I won't fall for your lies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I promise I have no idea what you're on about. If you explain, maybe I can help you?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: It's those frogs on the other side of the oasis! They're out to get us! All of us! If someone doesn't kill them all, we'll be living our lives as their slaves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... How about I head over there and take a look?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You want to go near them? Well then you're a fool! I will watch from a safe distance as they dine on your corpse!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then... Well provided that doesn't happen, I'll be back shortly to let you know what I've found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm just going to go now. You clearly have some... issues that you need to get on top of.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: That's it! You walk away! Just remember, I know the truth!

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue again

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello again! Have you found out what peace conditions the grumpy guts has?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Well don't go taking too long.

lines.speaker: Cuthbert
lines.text: I hear you're the one handling the peace talks. You will fail. There is no peace to be had with that man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd still like to give it a good go.

lines.speaker: Cuthbert
lines.text: So be it.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Hello there! You're the one sorting out the peace talks, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Frog
lines.text: Oh, hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... You can talk?

lines.speaker: Frog
lines.text: Well of course we can talk, silly. Why wouldn't we be able to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I guess I'd just never considered it before...

lines.speaker: Frog
lines.text: We live in a world where giant lizards with wings fly around and expel highly combustible fluids from their throats, but you never considered a frog could talk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well when you put it like that...

lines.speaker: Frog
lines.text: Well now that we've established our remarkable linguistic capabilities, how about some introductions. I'm Sue and this is Gary. Over there you have Dave and Jane, and good old Mr Lonesome over there is Cuthbert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you. I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Well, [player name], what can we do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's a guy over there that thinks you're all evil.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Evil? We're not evil! We're just on strike.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, what?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: We're on strike. Fair work requires fair treatment, and we've had enough of his nonsense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... What do you do? What are you striking from?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oh... Well I can't really remember. It's been so long that I've gone and totally forgotten. Hey, Gary, why are we on strike?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: No idea. I've been on strike my whole life, just like my pa, and his pa before him. At this point it's kind of a family tradition.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I know it's because of him though. That monster spends every day mistreating us. If he ever wants to see any work from us, he needs to start showing some basic respect.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Too right, Gary, too right. What reason does he have for thinking we're evil anyway?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well he's one of them capitalists, isn't he. They see a worker demanding fair rights and they go loopy. Can't have anything getting in the way of their profit margins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, he never mentioned anything about work. He seems to think you're out to overthrow humanity.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Overthrow humanity? What a load of nonsense! We love humans! Gary here had one as a pet once.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Ah, those were the days. I always loved my Sentia. Such a shame about the accident...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: It wasn't your fault, Gary. It wasn't your fault. Anyway, if this chap thinks we're plotting to overthrow humanity, what's he planning to do about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not too sure. He did mention at one point something about killing you all...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: He wants to what?!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: He wants to kill us?! Well now I think we should overthrow humanity!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... No...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Yeah, we've been treated like second class citizens for too long. The revolution starts today!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! How about peace?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Peace? Interesting... I suppose peace could be fun. There would need to be some assurances though. Wouldn't want the guy going back on his word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Well how about I go and ask him what conditions he'd be willing to accept.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Well that just sounds swell!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back shortly.

sections.title: Talking to Marcellus after talking to Gary and Sue

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: I'm only going to negotiate with the leader of those monsters. You go tell them that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I'm sorting it.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: The frogs didn't kill you? This is unexpected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I had a chat with them, and they're open to engaging in some peace talks.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Peace? The hypocrisy! After everything they've done they think peace is an option? Absolutely not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... But what exactly have they done that means peace isn't an option?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Oh... well...

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Well, you know...

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: They just...

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Look, I can't remember. But they must have done something!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Tetamo! I know! My brain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I'm starting to think that's where the problem lies as well.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: They must have taken my brain! That's why I can't remember! You tell them I want my brain back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd be willing to negotiate peace in exchange for your... brain?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Negotiate? I don't negotiate with monsters!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think they're monsters. They actually seem somewhat friendly.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Well do they have a leader?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Well you go tell them that I'll only negotiate with their leader.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Every day I find myself further questioning my life choices. Fine. I'll go and ask them about their leader.

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue again

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello again! How are things going with that lily pad sabotage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Just be careful of Dave and Jane. They're sticklers for the rules, I'm afraid. You can't let them see you.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello again! Have you found out what peace conditions the grumpy guts has?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. He seems open to the idea of peace talks, but he will only negotiate with your leader. Do you have a leader?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oh, well not really.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Nah, we're more about collective leadership. We did consider starting a union at one point, but Dave messed up the paperwork.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well would you be interested in having a leader?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: A change to our political structure? That's quite the thing to undergo.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Yeah, especially in times of war. Still, maybe we need to mix things up. We've been saying for a while now that we've been stagnating. Perhaps this is just the thing we need.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: There would need to be an election of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a process for that sort of thing?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Absolutely! We use a first past the post system where everyone votes based on a candidate's hopping capabilities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come again?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oh it's all very simple. The candidates perform their finest hop and everyone else votes on which they thought was best. The one with the most votes wins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well, I don't, but for the sake of my sanity, let's say I do. Shall we call an election then?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Sounds like a wonderful idea! There is just the small matter of Cuthbert...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cuthbert?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: That's right. You see, while you were gone, we told the others about this whole killing us all business. Now, I must admit, emotions did run a bit high, especially for Cuthbert.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Once he learns about the election, he will almost definitely want to put himself forward, and I'm afraid he has little interest in peace.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: I mean, can you blame the guy? Cuthbert was once the leader of the Frogs for Humanity society. The guy loved humans, but after what they did to his favourite plant...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Some wounds never heal...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're saying Cuthbert can't win the election?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: The idea of rigging an election is one that fills my democracy-loving heart with terror, but yes, we will need to do that.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: It's okay if we're the ones to do it, right?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Not in the slightest, but I do love that positive attitude. Besides, the alternative is all-out war with humanity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which we very much need to avoid! So how do we ensure Cuthbert can't win the election?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: The hop-off will see the candidates hop from the shore to a lily pad. Now, if Cuthbert's lily pad had an unfortunate hole in it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I guess I'm off to sabotage a lily pad.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Just be careful of Dave and Jane. They're sticklers for the rules, I'm afraid. You can't let them see you.

lines.speaker: Cuthbert
lines.text: It seems we are to have an election. Know that once I win, our war with humanity will be unlike anything ever seen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Cuthbert
lines.text: You should go. Go and be with your loved ones. Hold them close while you can. Soon you will never have that luxury again.

sections.title: Attempting to sabotage the lily pad

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: Hey, don't be going near those lily pads. We've got them all set up ready for the hop-off.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Hello there! Have you heard about the election? Isn't it thrilling?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is definitely a word that could be used to describe this situation. Anyway, speaking of the election, I need to inspect the lily pads.

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: No can do. We've got them all set up nicely. Can't be letting anyone mess things up, can we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course not, but don't you think it would be wise for a third party just to give them a quick once over? Besides, the two of you deserve a break.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Well I really could do with a bit of a rest, not to mention the fact that I'm starving. Still, the lily pads are our duty. It would be wrong of us to let someone else touch them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... You said you were hungry? Out of interest, what would you fancy? For when the election is out of the way, of course.

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: Oh I'd kill for a nice tasty orange!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An orange? I didn't know frogs ate oranges...

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Yeah most people think we just eat flies. We do, and we love them, but sometimes you find that you've just had enough flies and fancy something more exotic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you get your oranges?

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: They grow on trees around here, like this one right next to us. From time to time an orange will fall off, but the really good ones are the ones at the top.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Just a shame we can't reach those ones...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

sections.title: Talking to Dave or Jane again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me, where do you get your oranges?

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: They grow on trees around here, like this one right next to us. From time to time an orange will fall off, but the really good ones are the ones at the top.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Just a shame we can't reach those ones...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

sections.title: After chopping down the orange tree
Talking to Dave or Jane after chopping down the orange tree

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Can't stop to talk! There's oranges to be had!

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: Look! Oranges!

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue after sabotaging the lily pad

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello again! How are things going with that lily pad sabotage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all sorted.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: And just in time as well. The hop-off is about to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So who's Cuthbert's competition?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Sue of course!

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Don't worry. Once I win, I'll make sure these peace talks go nice and smoothly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then shall we get started?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Let's!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Welcome, one and all, to what is bound to be a thrilling frog hop-off! The stakes are high for this one, and indeed the future of our entire world may well be at stake.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Our first contender for today is Sue! Let's see what she's got!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: What a corker of a hop! Excellent work!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Next up, we have Cuthbert!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well, he's dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, what?!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Yeah, totally slipped my mind, but he never learnt how to swim!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A frog that never learnt to swim? Are you serious right now?!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Afraid so! Cuthbert is now as dead as an Old One. With only one contender left standing, I can now announce that Sue is the winner!

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue again

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hello! How go the peace talks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Remember, we kill flies, we get oranges. That's the deal.

sections.title: Talking to Dave or Jane after the hop-off

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Can you believe Cuthbert died? Such a shame.

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: Sue will be a great leader though. I can't wait to see what she does first!

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Well now that's all sorted, we can get these peace talks underway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe we killed him...

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Ah, don't worry yourself. Something like this was bound to happen at some point. It's his fault really. Imagine never learning to swim!

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Exactly! So what does our foe want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He thinks you've stolen his brain and would like it back.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: His brain? But we don't have his brain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well is there anything else you can offer him?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oh... Er... Not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be something.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: It's not like we really do much. We're frogs. All we do is eat flies, along with the odd orange.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe we can still work with that. I'm sure our friend doesn't like flies. Maybe you could deal with the flies around here for him?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Sounds like a job for us! Though we'd of course need something in return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Fair pay for fair work, of course. If not, we'll go on strike! We're good at going on strike.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how would you like to be paid?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Well normally I'd say in flies, but it seems we've got that covered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oranges?

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oranges! Perfect!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh, and orange juice as well! Straight from the tap!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From the tap? But orange juice doesn't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind. Let's not complicate things further. I'll take these terms to him and see what he says.

sections.title: Talking to Marcellus after talking to Gary and Sue

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You're back. Do you have the proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Then return to me once you have it.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Have you found the leader of those frogs? I'm only going to negotiate with the leader!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the frogs have selected a leader and she has sent me with her terms.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Well what are they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The frogs will kill the flies around the oasis and in return they'd like you to pay them in oranges.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Flies? But what have the flies done wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well don't they spread disease and eat your food?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Yes, but so did my brother, and I never wanted him dead. What else have the flies been doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know! You seemed convinced the frogs were out to get you before, maybe the flies are as well!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: The flies are out to get me? Is it really true?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what, sure. The flies want you dead and they plan to do it tonight.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: The monsters!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you willing to make this deal with the frogs?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Not yet. First I must see proof of this horrific betrayal by the flies. Find this proof for me, and I will agree to the deal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe my whole life is just one big nightmare. Fine, I'll find this proof.

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue again

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello! Did you find something to steal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Keep looking. I'm sure you'll find something suitable.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello! How go the peace talks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's open to the proposals, but he wants proof that the flies are against him.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: He doesn't half complain, does he? Maybe it would have just been easier to kill him...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: How about you steal a prized possession of his and plant it on the flies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh good, now we're adding theft to today's crimes.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well it could be worse. It could be murder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We already committed murder.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Nah, that wasn't murder! Manslaughter at best! Or would that be frogslaughter...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: It had to be done. Now, why don't you go and find something to steal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine.

sections.title: Opening the chest after unlocking it
Talking to Gary or Sue with the plushy

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello! Did you find something to steal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have a plushy here of his.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Perfect! Shove it in that big pile of dung the flies keep gathering around and then go and let him know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Poo! How wonderful!

lines.speaker: Cuthbert, Lord of Dread
lines.text: You! Prepare to face the Lord of Dread!

sections.title: Opening the chest after planting the evidence
Talking to Dave, Jane, or Marcellus before defeating Cuthbert

lines.speaker: Cuthbert
lines.text: I have come for you, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue before defeating Cuthbert

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello! How goes the planting of evidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done, but I do now have a little problem to deal with. I'll be back.

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue after defeating Cuthbert

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello! How goes the planting of evidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? Time to go and let him know!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Leave me. I need time to contemplate this great loss. Return to the frogs. Tell them we have a deal.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You're back. Do you have the proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I do. You see that big pile of dung the flies are gathered around? I'm sure I saw something belonging to you in it.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: It cannot be?! Wait here! I must check at once!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: I see my worst fears have come to pass. This is dark day not just for me, but for all of humanity.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Return to the frogs. Tell them we have a deal.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: I hear the peace talks are going well. We might be close to ending the conflict with that nasty human.

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: I do hope so. This war is starting to take a toll on all of us. We had to start rationing the oranges!

sections.title: Talking to Gary or Sue after Marcellus accepts the terms

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have good news! The terms have been accepted.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Wonderful! Peace at last!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, and it turns out Cuthbert was still alive after all.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Cuthbert's alive? Amazing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... no. He's now properly dead.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: War always has casualties. We will forever remember Cuthbert's sacrifice.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well said! Today will be a day remembered by all of frog-kind, and much of it is thanks to you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I only wish I had the ability to scrub the memories of all this from my brain, but as I can't, I guess I now have to carry them forever.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Have you tried soap and water?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oddly enough, no.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Oh don't be so negative. You've done a good thing, [player name]! Now go. Go with the blessing of the frogs, and know that you really are a true hero!

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Hello again. You helped forge my alliance with the frogs against those disgusting flies. Please feel free to use my farming patch in return. It's the least I can do.

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Look who it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Amazing! We get paid to eat all day, and our payment is more food. Life has never been better!

lines.speaker: Sue
lines.text: Too right, Gary! This is much better than being on strike!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

lines.speaker: Dave
lines.text: Today is a good day to be a frog!

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: It sure is. Now that we have peace, life is at an all time high!
